title: These are the Top 10 Emerging Technologies 2026
gist: The WEF's 2026 emerging technology list is dominated by three structural shifts: decentralized energy and production, radically personalized medicine, and quantum-resistant security infrastructure. Many of the ten technologies have cleared lab stage and are actively commercializing, signaling near-term investment windows rather than speculative bets. Convergence across energy storage, materials science, biotech, and AI is the defining architecture of this cycle.

insights.claim: Everything-to-grid (V2G and distributed battery) technology enabled 16,000 California solar homes to collectively inject 51 MW back into the grid during a single evening peak in 2024, exceeding the output of several fossil-fuel peaker plants without emissions — demonstrating that distributed assets can displace thermal generation infrastructure at scale.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Peaker plant operators and utility-scale gas generation face structural demand erosion; distributed energy resource (DER) aggregators and EV charging network operators hold asymmetric upside as grid monetization matures.
insights.quote: more than 16,000 solar-equipped homes linked into a distributed electricity network pushed 51 megawatts back to the grid during one such evening demand peak in 2024

insights.claim: Passive radiative cooling materials that reflect 95% of sunlight — deployable as paints, coatings, or building films — can cut energy use by up to 20% in commercial settings, with mandates already enacted in California and China, creating a large retrofit addressable market.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Building materials and energy efficiency plays (insulation, cool-roof products) gain regulatory tailwind; UK startup AssetCool's power-cable cooling approach that increases cable throughput by 30% is a differentiated grid infrastructure angle.
insights.quote: Suppliers report energy saving of up to 20% in settings such as grocery and retail stores

insights.claim: Direct lithium extraction (DLE) can reduce lithium production time from up to two years (traditional brine evaporation) to hours, while working with geothermal fluids, oilfield wastewater, and recycled materials — directly challenging China's 75% concentration of current lithium production.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: DLE is a direct geopolitical hedge against Chinese lithium supply dominance; investors in DLE technology companies or operators in Argentina, US, and Australia are positioned ahead of supply-chain diversification mandates from Western governments and automakers.
insights.quote: Three-quarters of lithium production is currently concentrated in China. Direct lithium extraction (DLE) offers a faster, flexible, and potentially more sustainable alternative

insights.claim: DLE's ability to extract lithium from oilfield wastewater creates an unexpected convergence between legacy oil and gas infrastructure and the clean energy transition, potentially giving oilfield services companies a new revenue stream and extending asset life.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Oilfield services incumbents (e.g., SLB, Halliburton) or their customers operating in lithium-brine-adjacent geologies could pivot wastewater streams into battery-grade lithium — a non-obvious value creation opportunity within existing asset bases.

insights.claim: Novel PFAS destruction methods — superheated water, electrical currents, UV-driven reactions — have advanced to operational commercial scale, with a Michigan facility running since 2023 and Daikin Industries achieving 99.99% PFAS destruction in field trials, shifting liability for PFAS contamination from theoretical to actionable.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Companies holding PFAS remediation liability (municipalities, industrial operators, defense contractors) face increasing cost certainty as destruction technology scales; PFAS remediation pure-plays and water treatment companies gain a large and growing addressable market as regulation tightens globally.
insights.quote: Daikin Industries, one of the world's largest PFAS producers, reported that its UV-based method successfully destroyed 99.99% of PFAS during a field trial

insights.claim: Personalized mRNA cancer vaccines combined with immunotherapy delivered a 40-50% reduction in melanoma recurrence or death risk versus immunotherapy alone in a recent South Carolina trial, providing clinical validation that tumor-specific mRNA constructs can materially improve outcomes beyond current standard-of-care.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: mRNA oncology platform companies (e.g., Moderna, BioNTech) have a validated clinical proof-point that extends the mRNA modality well beyond infectious disease; personalized oncology manufacturing and tumor sequencing infrastructure are critical bottlenecks and investment vectors.
insights.quote: patients receiving a personalized mRNA vaccine alongside immunotherapy saw a 40-50% reduction in the risk of recurrence or death compared to immunotherapy alone

insights.claim: Exosome-based drug delivery overcomes the core failure mode of targeted therapies (degradation or immune deflection in vivo) by exploiting the body's own intercellular communication system; a Phase 1 trial in the US stabilized pancreatic cancer patients with no remaining treatment options.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Exosome delivery platforms could unlock the commercial potential of gene editing and RNA therapeutics that have stalled due to delivery problems — a force multiplier for the broader precision medicine stack; early-stage exosome companies are acquisition targets for large pharma with delivery-challenged pipelines.
insights.quote: pancreatic cancer patients with no remaining treatment options were stabilized thanks to engineered exosomes targeting a previously hard-to-treat mutation

insights.claim: Quantum simulation of molecular interactions, exemplified by a 2025 IBM-Moderna collaboration running one of the largest protein-folding and mRNA interaction simulations to date, could structurally reduce the 90% clinical trial failure rate in drug development by improving pre-clinical candidate selection.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Quantum computing's near-term commercial case rests more on pharmaceutical simulation ROI than on cryptography disruption; pharma-quantum partnerships are an early signal of where quantum hardware providers will find their first large enterprise contracts.
insights.quote: a 2025 collaboration between IBM and Moderna, which used quantum computing to run one of the largest simulations of protein folding (creating proteins) and mRNA interactions to date
insights.locator: Quantum simulation for drug discovery section

themes.theme: Alternative Protein & Biofermentation as Industrial Substitution

insights.claim: Precision fermentation has moved beyond food into cosmetic peptides, pharmaceutical compounds, and chemicals traditionally derived from fossil fuels — meaning the technology platform is a broad industrial substitution play, not merely an alternative protein story.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Investors framing precision fermentation solely as a food-tech or ESG play are underpricing its industrial chemicals and specialty materials applications, where margins are structurally higher than in food and the incumbents (petrochemicals) have no natural defense.
insights.quote: the technology is also helping to create cosmetic peptides, pharmaceutical compounds and chemicals traditionally derived from fossil fuels

insights.claim: World models — AI systems trained on video, sensors, and text to build internal simulations of physical reality — represent a qualitative shift from pattern recognition to causal reasoning, enabling robots and climate models to generalize to novel situations without direct training data.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: NVIDIA's Cosmos platform is an early infrastructure layer for the physical AI stack; companies providing high-quality physical-world training data (video, sensor logs, real-world interaction datasets) are a non-obvious upstream bottleneck as world model adoption scales in robotics and autonomous systems.
insights.quote: NVIDIA's Cosmos platform trains robots on vast amounts of physical-world data so they can adapt to new, unfamiliar environments by relying on an internal model of how things work

insights.claim: Lattice-based cryptography has already entered mass-market deployment — protecting Apple's iMessage and planned for Google's Android — making post-quantum encryption an active infrastructure migration rather than a future contingency, years before fault-tolerant quantum computers arrive.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: The post-quantum cryptography migration is underway now, not in 10 years; cybersecurity vendors, PKI infrastructure providers, and HSM manufacturers that support NIST-standardized post-quantum algorithms (including lattice-based) are in an upgrade cycle with enterprise and government customers.
insights.quote: It already safeguards Apple's iMessage, and Google plans to include it in Android, alongside other encryption techniques

insights.claim: The 'harvest now, decrypt later' threat — where adversaries collect encrypted data today to decrypt with future quantum computers — means financial institutions, defense contractors, and critical infrastructure operators face a non-deferrable migration timeline regardless of when quantum computers mature.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Cybersecurity budget line items for post-quantum migration are becoming mandatory capex for regulated industries; this is a multi-year, non-discretionary spending cycle independent of broader IT budget pressures.
insights.quote: Decoding reams of encrypted internet traffic may not be possible today, but could be well within the reach of future quantum computers as the technology improves

insights.claim: The WEF's selection framework explicitly filters for technologies at 'the moment when decisions made by governments, industry, and research institutions will meaningfully shape how they arrive in the world' — making this list a leading indicator of near-term policy, regulatory, and procurement inflection points rather than long-horizon speculation.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: The list functions as a forward procurement and regulation signal: technologies appearing here historically attract government grant programs, regulatory fast-tracks, and large enterprise pilot budgets within 12-24 months of publication.
insights.quote: they are chosen for the signals that suggest they are approaching the moment when decisions made by governments, industry, and research institutions will meaningfully shape how they arrive in the world

insights.claim: All ten technologies share a 'more for less' efficiency orientation — cooling without electricity, drugs without animals, lithium without long evaporation — suggesting the dominant commercial thesis of this technology cycle is input cost compression rather than new capability creation, which historically compresses margins for incumbent input suppliers.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: The macro investment implication is deflationary for categories being substituted (fossil-fuel chemicals, animal agriculture inputs, thermal peaker generation, traditional lithium mining) and inflationary for the enabling infrastructure layers (fermentation capacity, DLE equipment, grid software, mRNA manufacturing).
insights.quote: They achieve more for less, whether it's space cooling without electricity, food production without animal farming, or substantially reducing the cost of traditional drug discovery
insights.locator: Three trends for new tech section
